___
📄 FORMAT & TOOLS
──────────────────────────────
• Write your thesis using either Microsoft Word or Overleaf (LaTeX).
• Two Overleaf templates have been shared for your reference 
• These templates are guides only; not a strict format. Your content must follow the guidelines shared separately (the Thesis Guide in the dashboard).

──────────────────────────────
📑 THESIS STRUCTURE & CHAPTERS
──────────────────────────────
Your thesis must include the following chapters in order:

0. Title Page
1. Declaration 
3. SPER Form (Signed) — appended at the very beginning of the document as a physical page, NOT as a link or appendix.
3. Abstract
4. Acknowledgements 
5. Table of Contents — must include all chapters and subsections
6. List of Figures
7. List of Tables
8. List of Abbreviations (include this section only if abbreviations are used)
9. Introduction
10. Literature Review
11. Methodology
    → Between Methodology and Results, you have the flexibility to include additional chapters (e.g., Dataset Preparation, Model Implementation, Model Evaluation, etc.) based on the weight and nature of your work.
12. Results
13. Discussion (Results and Discussion can be combined as one chapter as well)
14. Conclusion
    → Include a Reflection subsection within the Conclusion chapter (not as a separate chapter)
15. References
16. Appendices
    • Appendix 1: Link to your code repository (do NOT copy the entire code into the thesis)
    • Appendix 2 onwards: Any other additional supporting material

──────────────────────────────
📏 WORD COUNT & FORMATTING
──────────────────────────────
• Word count: 20,000 words (±10%), i.e., between 18,000 and 22,000 words.
• The word count applies from the Introduction chapter through to the end of the Conclusion chapter only. References and Appendices are excluded.
• Use proper line spacing to keep your thesis within 80 –100 pages. Do not exceed 100 pages.
• All chapters must have subsections, and all subsections must appear in the Table of Contents.

──────────────────────────────
🖼️ FIGURES, TABLES & ABBREVIATIONS
──────────────────────────────
• All images and tables must be clearly labelled (e.g., Figure 1.1, Table 2.1).
• All labelled figures and tables must be included in the List of Figures and List of Tables respectively.
• If your thesis contains abbreviations, include a List of Abbreviations placed after the List of Tables.

──────────────────────────────
📚 REFERENCING
──────────────────────────────
• Use RGU Harvard referencing style consistently throughout.
• This applies to both in-text citations and the bibliography/reference list.
• Inconsistent or missing references will affect your grade.

──────────────────────────────
💾 SUBMISSION
──────────────────────────────
• Save your thesis as a PDF file using your RGU student number only as the filename (e.g., 1234567.pdf).
• Upload your submission via the Moodle submission link provided.

──────────────────────────────
✅ QUICK CHECKLIST BEFORE YOU SUBMIT
──────────────────────────────
□ Signed SPER form inserted at the beginning (not as a link/appendix)
□ All required chapters included with subsections
□ Flexibility chapters (if any) placed between Methodology and Results
□ Reflection included as a subsection under Conclusion
□ Word count is within 18,000 – 22,000 words (Introduction to Conclusion only)
□ Thesis is within 80–100 pages
□ All figures and tables labelled and listed
□ List of Abbreviations included (if applicable)
□ RGU Harvard referencing applied consistently
□ Code repository link in Appendix 1 (no full code copied)
□ File saved as your RGU number only
□ Uploaded via Moodle
